source_pr.title: store/tikv: Support forwarding via follower when the region's leader is unreachable
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: MyonKeminta <MyonKeminta@users.noreply.github.com>

<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary:

Currently a region may be unavailable when the leader is network-isolated to the TiDB. In this case, we can try forwarding the request via a follower.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed: Once the config `tikvclient.enable-redirection` is enabled, when it cannot connect to a TiKV node to access a region's leader, it will try asking a follower to forward the request to the leader.

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch
  - Maybe 4.0
- Requires merging #23362 first

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests (Not yet done)<!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test 
- Integration test

Side effects

- Performance regression
    - Consumes more CPU
    - Consumes more MEM
- Breaking backward compatibility

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- Support forwarding region requests to the leader via a follower in case there's network isolation.

symptoms: Replace failpoint with hook to avoid unstable tests
failure_signature: Replace failpoint with hook to avoid unstable tests
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to shared/global state pollution: it relied on a global failpoint (`mockRequestLiveness`) to control request liveness behavior, which is inherently unstable when tests run concurrently or when failpoint state leaks across tests. This PR stabilizes it by replacing the global failpoint with a per-instance testing hook (`RegionCache.testingKnobs.mockRequestLiveness`), making the mocked liveness behavior local to the test and reducing cross-test interference.
fix_pattern: Replace global failpoint-based mocking with a per-instance testing hook (RegionCache testing knobs) to isolate state per test and avoid cross-test interference.

evidence: evidence: Replace failpoint with hook to avoid unstable tests
